segments.text: "Then David reviewed his troops and appointed over them commanders of hundreds and of thousands.
"He sent out the troops, a third under Joab, a third under Joab’s brother Abishai son of Zeruiah, and a third under Ittai the Gittite. And the king said to the troops,
I will surely march out with you as well.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: "But the people pleaded,

You must not go out! For if we have to flee, they will pay no attention to us. Even if half of us die, they will not care; but you are worth ten thousand of us. It is better for now if you support us from the city.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: I will do whatever seems best to you,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king replied. So he stood beside the gate, while all the troops marched out by hundreds and by thousands.
"Now the king had commanded Joab, Abishai, and Ittai,
Treat the young man Absalom gently for my sake.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: And all the people heard the king’s orders to each of the commanders regarding Absalom.
"So David’s army marched into the field to engage Israel in the battle, which took place in the forest of Ephraim.
"There the people of Israel were defeated by David’s servants, and the slaughter was great that day—twenty thousand men.
"The battle spread over the whole countryside, and that day the forest devoured more people than the sword.

"Now Absalom was riding on his mule when he met the servants of David, and as the mule went under the thick branches of a large oak, Absalom’s head was caught fast in the tree. The mule under him kept going, so that he was suspended in midair.
"When one of the men saw this, he told Joab,
I just saw Absalom hanging in an oak tree!
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: You just saw him!
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Joab exclaimed.
Why did you not strike him to the ground right there? I would have given you ten shekels of silver and a warrior’s belt!
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: "The man replied,

Even if a thousand shekels of silver were weighed out into my hands, I would not raise my hand against the son of the king. For we heard the king command you and Abishai and Ittai, ‘Protect the young man Absalom for my sake.’
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: If I had jeopardized my own life—and nothing is hidden from the king—you would have abandoned me.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: "But Joab declared,
I am not going to wait like this with you!
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: And he took three spears in his hand and thrust them through the heart of Absalom while he was still alive in the oak tree.
"And ten young men who carried Joab’s armor surrounded Absalom, struck him, and killed him.
"Then Joab blew the ram’s horn, and the troops broke off their pursuit of Israel because Joab had restrained them.
"They took Absalom, cast him into a large pit in the forest, and piled a huge mound of stones over him. Meanwhile, all the Israelites fled, each to his home.
"During his lifetime, Absalom had set up for himself a pillar in the King’s Valley, for he had said,
I have no son to preserve the memory of my name.
segments.speaker: Absalom

segments.text: So he gave the pillar his name, and to this day it is called Absalom’s Monument.
"Then Ahimaaz son of Zadok said,
Please let me run and tell the king the good news that the LORD has avenged him of his enemies.
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: "But Joab replied,
You are not the man to take good news today. You may do it another day, but you must not do so today, because the king’s son is dead.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: "So Joab said to a Cushite,
Go, tell the king what you have seen.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: The Cushite bowed to Joab and took off running.
"Ahimaaz son of Zadok, however, persisted and said to Joab,
Regardless of whatever may happen, please let me also run behind the Cushite!
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: My son,
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Joab replied,
why do you want to run, since you will not receive a reward?
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: No matter what, I want to run!
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: he replied.
Then run!
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text:
Joab told him.
So Ahimaaz ran by way of the plain and outran the Cushite.
"Now David was sitting between the two gates when the watchman went up to the roof of the gateway by the wall, looked out, and saw a man running alone.
"So he called out and told the king.
If he is alone,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king replied,
he bears good news.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: As the first runner drew near,
"the watchman saw another man running, and he called out to the gatekeeper,
Look! Another man is running alone!
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: This one also brings good news,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: said the king.
"The watchman said,
The first man appears to me to be running like Ahimaaz son of Zadok.
segments.speaker: The watchman

segments.text: This is a good man,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: said the king.
He comes with good news.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: "Then Ahimaaz called out to the king,
All is well!
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text:
And he bowed facedown before the king.
He continued,
Blessed be the LORD your God! He has delivered up the men who raised their hands against my lord the king.
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: "The king asked,
Is the young man Absalom all right?
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: And Ahimaaz replied,
When Joab sent the king’s servant and your servant, I saw a great tumult, but I do not know what it was.
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: Move aside,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: said the king,
and st and here.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: So he stepped aside.
"Just then the Cushite came and said,
May my lord the king hear the good news: Today the LORD has avenged you of all who rose up against you!
segments.speaker: Just then the Cushite came and

segments.text: "The king asked the Cushite,
Is the young man Absalom all right?
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: And the Cushite replied,
May what has become of the young man happen to the enemies of my lord the king and to all who rise up against you to harm you.
segments.speaker: The Cushite

segments.text: "The king was shaken and went up to the gate chamber and wept. And as he walked, he cried out,
O my son Absalom! My son, my son Absalom! If only I had died instead of you, O Absalom, my son, my son!
segments.speaker: The Cushite
